converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Glenn H. Beyer papers
Beyer, Glenn H.
53-4-1548
10.3 cubic feet. 10.3 cubic feet.
1934-1969. Manuscripts, gallery proofs, correspondence, questionnaires, photographs, research material, notes, printed materials, film strips, monographs, and articles. Manuscripts and galley proofs; correspondence; questionnaires; photographs, negatives, and slides; research material, notes, and rough drafts pertaining to rural housing; printed materials; and 2 victrola records and 3 film strips relating to the 1950 census. Collection material in English 2861252
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
September 1990
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, March 2003
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, September 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Glenn Beyer was born in Chester, South Dakota in 1913. He earned degrees from Augustana (South Dakota) College in 1935 and George Washington University in 1939. He held positions in several federal housing agencies until he became Professor of Housing and Design at Cornell University in 1947. He became Director of the Cornell Center for Housing and Environmental Studies (formerly the Housing Research Center) upon its establishment in 1950. Beyer wrote several books, monographs, and articles on rural and farm housing and housing for the elderly. Many works were concerned with Upstate New York housing practices.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Manuscripts and galley proofs; correspondence; questionnaires; photographs, negatives, and slides; research material, notes, and rough drafts pertaining to rural housing; printed materials; and 2 victrola records and 3 film strips relating to the 1950 census.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Chronological Correspondence Files, 1955-1969
Series II. Subject Files
Series III. Photographs
Series IV. Printed Material
Series V. Miscellany
Cite As:
Glenn H. Beyer papers, #53-4-1548. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Digitized copy of "The Cornell Kitchen" can be found online here: https://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/40835
Related Materials
See also collections #23-2-2817 and #47-10-3394 for additional biographical information.
Color slides. Questionnaires. Photographs. Negatives. Motion pictures (visual works). Galley proofs. College teachers. Older people -- Dwellings. Housing, Rural. Housing -- United States -- Research. Housing -- New York (State) Beyer, Glenn H. Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University. Cornell Center for Housing and Environmental Studies Cornell University. Housing Research Center
